title: Image 19 of The Washington times (Washington [D.C.]), December 21, 1934
raw_ocr: U SLOAN CURB FACES FIGHT ON HILL By FRANKLIN G SARTWELL Congressional opposition which Is rapidly developing to the ef forts of the Home Owners Loan Corporation to go out of business is giving the Administration a sorry foretaste of the difficulties it will encounter when and if it reaches the point of liquidating the many recently created emer gency organizations Although the corporation an nounced about a month ago that it has spent all its moneythat is that it had loaned it and that it believed it had covered the field for which it was created and therefore that it should discon tinue its loaning operations it was Indicated here today that it will not be permitted to be the first of the New Deal agencies to go out of business More Loans Sought Determined se n tim ent fyas sprung up in congressional circles to keep the corporation going and going as a lending agency reach ing into hundreds of thousands of homes This sentiment probably will take the form of bills which would appropriate at least an other thousand million dollars for the corporation to lend to citizens desiring to preserve their equities in their homes The
